Also, when I try to run qsynaptics from the terminal, an error

    Can't detect hardware properties.
    This is normal if you are running linux kernel 2.6.
    Check the kernel log for touchpad hardware information.

appears. Actually, after error, qsynaptic runs, but it does not change anything.

Any help would be appreciated
Thanks

---

### Post by orawax on 2007-03-27
One step forward would be following the advice at Ubuntu Wiki Community docs:
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Peripherals#head-88ab01e09b1f8cb66733b224268e3aa1d30722ec](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Peripherals#head-88ab01e09b1f8cb66733b224268e3aa1d30722ec)

After this, I got qsynaptics to work (was unable to install gsynaptics in 64bit dapper), and touchpad seems to work a bit more reasonably. Actually I already thought this solved the problem totally, but I'm afraid the old symptoms are gradually re-emerging.

A band-aid cure, if nothing else seems to help, is to do:
sudo modprobe -r psmouse; sudo modprobe psmouse

This disables some of the features (scrolling for instance), but makes the touchpad work otherwise right.

---

### Post by aqshin on 2007-03-30
finally problem solved :)
here is the link:
[http://ubuntu.wordpress.com/2006/03/24/disable-synaptics-touchpad/](http://ubuntu.wordpress.com/2006/03/24/disable-synaptics-touchpad/)
